LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KHONG TU'S NOTICE OF SECOND SUPPLEMENTAL MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 23, 2012. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS COMPLAINANTS KHONG TU AND KHY LOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AND GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. THE FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE DEFICIENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Zacks, Esq., and James Brandan Kraus, Esq., from Zacks & Freedman, 415-956-8100, for defendants and moving party
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq., 415-563-9301, for cross-defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANTS KHONG TU AND KHY SOK LOR'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Granted. Plaintiff is required to pay attorney fees of $13,119.25 and costs of $490.00 to defendant. These fees and costs were reasonably incurred. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq., 415-563-9301, for Plaintiff and moving party
Andrew Zacks, Esq., and James Brandan Kraus, Esq., from Zacks & Freedman, 415-956-8100, for Defendants and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF ANDREW HAWKINS-COHE'S Motion For Reconsideration is Denied. After yet another full review of the parties' positions on defendant's anti-SLAPP motion, the court remains of the view that the motion was correctly granted. Defendant satisfied his burden that non-incidental allegations in each of plaintiff's claims arise out of activitoes protected by the anti-SLAPP statute and plaintiff has not shown that any of his claims have merit. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Nonstatutory Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To First Count Of The X-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Zacks, Esq., and James Brandan Kraus, Esq., from Zacks & Freedman, 415-956-8100, for cross-complainants and moving party
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq., 415-563-9301, for cross-defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS COMPLAINANTS KHONG TU AND KHY SOK LOR'S Motion For Nonstatutory Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To First Count Of The Cross-Complaint. Granted with ten days leave to file an amended answer. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege one or more affirmative defenses that subsequent to the foreclosure the lease he had prior to the foreclosure was reinstated and/or that the such lease called for payment of rent that was not substantially less than market rent. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
